data.description:

China Merchants Bank is the bookrunner and lead underwriter of the issuance.

data.title: India central bank's operations to keep overnight rates within policy corridor, analysts say
data.description:
The Indian central bank's measured approach to withdrawing banking system liquidity is likely to keep overnight interbank lending rates between the policy repo rate and the floor of the policy corridor, three analysts said on Friday, allowing some policy transmission.

The Reserve Bank of India withdrew 1 trillion rupees ($11.7 billion) from the banking system on Friday through a seven-day variable rate reverse repo, rolling over an operation from last week, despite a rising liquidity surplus.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

The RBI cut its policy rate by a steeper-than-expected 50 basis points last month, shifted its policy stance to 'neutral', and stopped liquidity infusions.

It then conducted a liquidity absorption operation on June 27 as overnight rates fell below its policy corridor.

India's policy repo rate, the mid-point of the corridor, stands at 5.50%, while the standing deposit facility (SDF) rate, the floor of the corridor, is at 5.25%.

Market participants are unsure whether the RBI wants to push overnight rates close to the policy rate or if it is comfortable with rates hovering near the SDF.

A spike in market rates could affect the transmission of rate cuts that have not been passed on to borrowers and impact economic growth.

MARKET REACTION

The weighted average call (WACR) and weighted average tri-party repo rates (WATR) dipped this week as India's liquidity surplus hit a three-year high at more than 4 trillion rupees.

On Friday, the weighted average call rate rose 5 bps to 5.30%, while the weighted average tri-party repo was at 5.23%, up 11 bps.

KEY QUOTES

"The consistent VRRRs are meant to ensure that the call money rate stays closer to SDF and not breach it. However, for it to trade around or higher than repo rate, the quantum needs to be higher," said Madhavi Arora, chief economist at Emkay Global Financial Services.

Harshal Joshi, fund manager, fixed income at Bandhan Asset Management said: "The only purpose of VRRR is that the overnight rates remain at least at SDF rate. I do not expect the RBI to push the overnight rates to repo, and would be satisfied if these rates remain below SDF and repo."

GRAPHIC

($1 = 85.3760 Indian rupees)

data.title: Japan's Nikkei nudges higher, but snaps three-week winning streak
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei ended slightly higher on Friday, as chip-related stocks tracked Wall Street's strong overnight performance, but the index snapped a three-week winning streak on caution ahead of the U.S. trade deal deadline.

The Nikkei was up 0.06% at 39,810.88, after hitting an intraday high of 40,012.66.

The benchmark index fluctuated between negative territory and modest gains throughout the session. For the week, the Nikkei slipped 0.85%, snapping a three-week winning streak.

The broader Topix was flat at 2,827.95.

"Investors remained optimistic about the market outlook, but the Nikkei is still vulnerable," said Shuutarou Yasuda, a market analyst at Tokai Tokyo Intelligence Laboratory.

"As soon as it hit the 40,000 level, there was a sell-off to book profits," he said.

Wall Street rallied on Thursday to record closing highs, as chipmaker Nvidia rose closer to a $4 trillion valuation and a surprisingly strong U.S. jobs report cheered investors, who shrugged off dimming chances for an interest rate cut this month.

Investors remained cautious and refrained from making active bets on Japanese stocks at the end of the week, as they awaited a key U.S. trade tariff deadline next week, strategists said.

Chip-related stocks led the gains on the Nikkei, with Advantest rising 1.38% and Tokyo Electron gaining 0.46%.

Banking shares advanced as Japanese government bond yields tracked U.S. yields higher. Solid job gains in the U.S. bolstered the case for the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates on hold.

The bank sector rose 1.29%, with shares of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group climbing 1.16%.

Utility sector (.IEPNG.T) rose 1.3% to become the top gainer among the 33 industry sub-indexes on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Uniqlo-brand owner Fast Retailing fell 0.6% to weigh on the Nikkei the most.

data.title: Australia shares score record close, post best week in a month
data.description:
Key points:
  • ASX200 closes at fresh peak, logs best week since early June
  • Miners fall 1%
  • NZ50 posts best week in two months

Australian shares closed at a fresh all-time high after a largely range-bound session on Friday, as gains in discretionary consumer, real estate and healthcare stocks were partly offset by losses in the heavyweight mining sector.

The S&P/ASX 200 index ended up 0.1% at a record close of 8,603 points, logging a 1% weekly rise, its strongest since early June.

Consumer discretionary stocks (.AXDJ) rose 0.8% on Friday and posted its strongest week since late April. Real estate stocks (.AXRE) also advanced 0.8% to its highest level since early December.

A rally in the two rate-sensitive sectors has continued into fiscal 2026 as the outlook for further rate cuts gains momentum, said Grady Wulff, senior market analyst at Bell Direct.

Lower rates are boosting consumer discretionary spending, and also making REITs more attractive by reducing financing costs and supporting higher dividends, she added.

Markets have priced in a 95.5% chance that the Reserve Bank of Australia will cut its cash rate by 25 basis points for a third time this year when it meets on Tuesday.

Healthcare stocks also gained 0.7% to hit a four-month high. Investors are buying into the undervalued sector at discounts, Wulff said.

The mining sub-index was the day's biggest laggard, declining 1%, after hitting a three-week high in the previous session, with BHP and Rio Tinto losing 1.4% and 1.3% respectively. The sector, however, posted its strongest week since mid-May.

"The move reflects growing caution around iron ore prices amid the renewed skepticism over China's growth outlook as Beijing finds itself in an increasingly tight spot on the global trade map," said Hebe Chen, market analyst at Vantage Markets.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index closed up 0.5% at 12,766.60 points, posting its strongest week since mid-May.

Investors are widely expecting the Reserve Bank of New Zealand to leave interest rates unchanged on Wednesday, a Reuters poll showed.

data.title: Switzerland Jobless Rate Steady at 2.7% in June
data.description:
The Swiss unemployment rate was steady at a non-seasonally adjusted 2.7% in June 2025, remaining at its lowest level since November 2024.

The number of unemployed persons fell by 1.1 thousand to a seven-month low of 126.9 thousand.

Meanwhile, the youth unemployment rate, measuring job-seekers between 15 and 24 years old, ticked up to 2.5% from 2.4% in May, with the number of young unemployed rising by 0.07 thousand to 10.7 thousand.

At the same time, the number of reported job vacancies increased by 1.6 thousand to 39.8 thousand positions during the period.

Adjusted for seasonal factors, the jobless rate edged up to 2.9% in June from 2.8% in the prior month.

data.title: Dollar Slips on Renewed Tariff Concerns
data.description:
The US dollar index slipped below 97 on Friday, snapping a two-day rally as renewed concerns over trade policy weighed on sentiment.

President Donald Trump announced plans to begin issuing letters on trade matters, potentially setting unilateral tariff rates ahead of the July 9 negotiation deadline—injecting fresh uncertainty into global markets.

At the same time, the House of Representatives passed Trump’s sweeping tax cuts and spending bill, which now heads to the White House.

The legislation is projected to expand the federal budget deficit by over $3 trillion, adding to longer-term fiscal risks.

Meanwhile, the US economy added 147,000 jobs in June, beating expectations of 110,000 and marking an increase from May’s 144,000.

The data helped ease recession concerns and reduced pressure on the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates in the near term.

data.title: Yum Brands in talks to merge its two Indian franchisees, Economic Times reports
data.description:
Yum Brands , which owns KFC and Pizza Hut, is in talks to merge its Indian franchisees Devyani International and Sapphire Foods , the Economic Times reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The talks could see Sapphire Foods merged into Devyani International via a share swap, with Sapphire shareholders potentially receiving one Devyani share for every three held, the newspaper reported.

The deal may involve Devyani acquiring Sapphire's KFC and Pizza Hut franchise rights, or consolidating all KFC franchisee stores in India under Devyani, the report added.

Devyani and Sapphire did not immediately respond to a request for comment, while U.S.-based Yum Brands was not available for comment outside regular business hours.

Shares of Sapphire Foods jumped nearly 9%, while Devyani International rose about 3% after the report, trading at 347.95 rupees and 172.25 rupees, respectively, as of 12:40 p.m. IST.

("Mila" or the Company")

Yarrol Gold Project Update

Mila Advances Yarrol Gold model with stage 1 Drilling

Mila Resources Plc , the post-discovery gold exploration accelerator, is pleased to report the results from the remaining six drillholes completed as part of the first phase of exploration programme at the Company's flagship Yarrol Gold Project.

The results reinforce the continuity and potential of the Yarrol mineralised system, prove further continuity of mineralised structures at depth and validate historic intercepts, with multiple intercepts supporting the presence of high-grade shoots.

Interpretation

Mila's maiden drilling campaign at the Yarrol Target, a ~2km area along the 20km Yarrol Fault, consisted of a 10-hole Reverse Circulation (RC) programme totalling 1,870m.

This first phase of drilling has delivered on the Company's three objectives, to:      

·    Confirm historical gold intercepts

·    Test extensions of the known mineralisation; and

·    Develop the Company's own geological model to guide future targeting.

The results returned consistent gold mineralisation across every hole, supporting the presence of a large, structurally controlled, system. This includes step-out holes that extend known zones along strike and at depth and infill holes that confirm historical grades and continuity.

Some standout intercepts from the campaign include:

  • ·      MYARC0195:

     - 2m @ 6.44g/t Au from 124m

     - 1m @ 5.19g/t Au from 117m

  • ·    MYARC0196:

     - 5m @ 2.68g/t Au from 69m- 5m @ 2.53g/t Au from 76m

  • ·      MYARC0197:

     - 11m @ 2.91g/t Au from 11m, incl. 1m @ 16.45g/t Au

     - 1m @ 39.10g/t Au from 32m

     - 17m @ 5.46g/t Au from 42m, incl. 8m @ 9.76g/t Au with 1m @ 47.40g/t Au

  • ·      MYARC0204:

     - 15m @ 1.76g/t Au from 101m, incl. 1m @ 16.05g/t Au

     - 4m @ 3.54g/t Au from 133m, incl. 1m @ 13.05g/t Au

     - 6m @ 1.19g/t Au from 152m

For further information on these results please refer Mila Resources News releases of 21/05/25 [RNS Number: 5654J] and 06/06/25 [RNS Number: 4878L] 

These are significant results that speak to the system's scale and its potential to host several high-grade shoots.

Importantly, many holes intersected multiple mineralised zones at similar depths highlighting strong vertical and lateral continuity, a key feature that demonstrates the potential large-scale of the deposit. The high-grade intercepts in both this drilling campaign and the historic drilling point to a valuable core to the system.

Mila will now undertake detailed structural and technical analysis to pinpoint the controls of the high-grade gold. The updated model from this work will be tested with a targeted diamond drilling, before further drilling is undertaken to complete a maiden JORC-compliant resource.

Next Phase of Work

As a first step, the Company is commissioning a specialist resource geology team to complete a comprehensive review of the existing dataset including recent drilling results. The insights gained will guide the design of the next phase of drilling, with the objective of progressing Yarrol towards a maiden JORC-compliant resource.

In parallel, the technical team is advancing a wide-ranging exploration programme that includes:

·    Structural mapping and geophysical interpretation to unlock the resource potential along the Yarrol Fault.

·    Extending ground magnetics across key structural trends to identify additional Yarrol-style anomalies.

·    Advancing Mila's two other Queensland assets, the Monal and Mt Steadman projects, both of which host historical, non-compliant resources and are being progressed to drill-ready status as part of upcoming work phases.

Mark Stephenson, Executive Director of Mila Resources, commented:

"This first phase of exploration has delivered beyond our expectations with strong gold intercepts across the drilling programme.

"The Yarrol system is holding together exceptionally well the more we drill, the more it grows. These latest results confirm strong continuity and high-grade zones, pointing to the scale and quality of the system. With a skilled technical team in place, we're now using structural modelling and diamond drilling to define controls on mineralisation and rapidly advance Yarrol toward a maiden resource."

Competent Person Statement

The information in this announcement relating to Exploration Results is based on information compiled by Neil Hutchison, who is Technical Director of Mila Resources, and a member of The Australasian Institute of Geoscientists. Mr Hutchison has sufficient experience relevant to the style of mineralisation and type of deposit under consideration, and to the activity he is undertaking to qualify as a Competent Person as defined in the 2012 Edition of the "Australasian Code for Reporting of Exploration Results, Mineral Resource and Ore Reserves".

data.title: Kanion Pharmaceutical Gets Nod to Trial Atopic Dermatitis Gel
data.description:
Jiangsu Kanion Pharmaceutical obtained the Chinese drug administration's approval to trial the chemical new drug KYS2301 gel, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

The gel will be tested as a treatment for atopic dermatitis.

The pharmaceutical company invested 44.9 million yuan into the research and development of the drug, the disclosure said.
